what_it_does: This file implements the Generate workspace surface: three tabbed views (Attune, Learn, Imagery) that let users set project preferences, play step-by-step tutorials, and create image artifacts. It delegates all state and resolution to global registries (MS_MEMORY, MS_RESOLVE, MS_TUTORIALS, MS_ARTIFACTS, MS_KINDS, MS_DECISIONS, MS_PRODUCE) and exposes a small helper API on window.MS_GENERATE for testing.
observations: All substantive logic is delegated to global window.* registries; this file only orchestrates UI and renders results.
React's useState is aliased to useStateGEN to avoid collisions in the global-script environment.
Attune questions each write a preference key via MS_MEMORY and immediately show how MS_RESOLVE resolves a matching kind.variable.
displayValue contains special sentinel handling so default/token values are shown as human labels from MS_DECISIONS rather than raw tokens.
Learn is a tutorial/show-through player with list selection, step navigation, progress dots, and target highlighting.
Imagery creates artifacts through MS_PRODUCE (falling back to MS_ARTIFACTS) using a context built from MS_VI_BRAIN if present.
window.useArtifacts && window.useArtifacts() is called inside the GENImagery render path to subscribe to artifact changes.
CandidateCard is used if available; otherwise a plain figure fallback is rendered.
standouts: The Attune tab makes the preference→resolution loop visible in real time.
Imagery claims to generate 'real' prompts/alt/palette through the same engine rather than fake placeholder art.
MS_GENERATE helpers are deliberately exposed on window for unit testing.
conventions: Follows the project's global registry/module pattern (window.MS_*).
Functional React components with hooks, JSX, and inline event handlers.
Uses guard patterns like window.MS_TOAST && window.MS_TOAST.show(...).
Prefers direct registry mutation (MS_MEMORY.setPreference) over local React state for persisted preferences.
concerns: Heavy reliance on globals means the file is not self-contained and will fail silently in places if registries are absent.
window.useArtifacts && window.useArtifacts() inside render can cause side effects on every render.
MS_PRODUCE || MS_ARTIFACTS fallback may hide that the preferred producer is missing.
displayValue's sentinel logic is intricate and depends on MS_DECISIONS internals that are not shown here.
No validation that tuts[0] exists before using it as a default in GENLearn.
notes: A thin but carefully-wired workspace that turns preference changes into visible resolution outcomes and routes image generation through the studio's shared artifact engine.
questions: What exact shape does MS_RESOLVE.variable return, and what does source:'default' mean?
How does MS_VI_BRAIN.genContext combine tweaks and explicit subject?
What is the contract of window.useArtifacts and does it return cleanup?
Are MS_DECISIONS.has/get/activeOption guaranteed to exist, or should they be guarded like MS_TOAST?
What does CandidateCard expect in its art, kind, and ctx props?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a surface that orchestrates Attune/Learn/Imagery while keeping logic in registries.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the studio's generative engine feel controllable and teachable: every preference visibly changes resolution, tutorials guide users, and imagery output is treated as a real artifact candidate rather than a mock.
novelty: The real-time 'learn → resolve' feedback loop in Attune, where each preference choice immediately demonstrates how a downstream variable resolves, is a distinct interaction pattern.
contribution: This file contributes a user-facing Generate workspace that binds preference capture, tutorial playback, and image artifact creation to the project's global registry system, plus a small testable helper API (window.MS_GENERATE) that encapsulates the resolution and display logic used by Attune.
summary_for_embedding: A React workspace component for a generative design studio that exposes Attune preference tuning, a tutorial player, and an image artifact generator, all backed by global registries and exposed helpers.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a single place to steer the studio's generative behavior through preferences, learn the system via tutorials, and produce real image artifacts resolved by the shared engine.

symbols.does: Resolves a single kind.variable using the current memory/context via MS_RESOLVE.
symbols.description: Given a question object, resolveDemo looks up the corresponding kind and variable in MS_KINDS and calls window.MS_RESOLVE.variable with an empty context, causing resolution to read from MS_MEMORY preferences.

symbols.does: Humanizes a resolved value for display, handling decision sentinels and option labels.
symbols.description: displayValue converts raw resolved tokens into readable labels. If the value looks like a default sentinel for a registered MS_DECISIONS decision, it returns the active option label; otherwise it maps the value to the question's own option label or formats it via MS_COMPOSE.

symbols.does: Writes a user's answer for a preference question into MS_MEMORY.
symbols.description: ask is a thin wrapper around window.MS_MEMORY.setPreference that stores the chosen value under the question's key, making the preference available to the resolution engine.

symbols.does: Renders the Attune tab: preference questions with live resolution feedback.
symbols.description: GENAttune lists QUESTIONS, renders option buttons, highlights the current preference, and below each question shows the resolved value and source for the associated kind.variable, calling bump() after each choice to refresh the UI.

symbols.does: Renders the Learn tab: a tutorial/show-through player.
symbols.description: GENLearn lists available tutorials from MS_TUTORIALS, lets the user select one, and displays the current step with title, body, target, progress dots, and Back/Next/Done navigation using local React state.

symbols.does: Switches the selected tutorial and resets the step to zero.
symbols.description: pick is a small local helper used by GENLearn's tutorial list buttons to update the selected tutorial id and reset step state so the new tutorial starts from the beginning.

symbols.does: Renders the Imagery tab: input, mood display, artifact grid, and generation trigger.
symbols.description: GENImagery shows a subject input, the current mood preference, a generate button, and a reverse-ordered grid of image artifacts. It calls window.useArtifacts if present and creates new artifacts via MS_PRODUCE or MS_ARTIFACTS.

symbols.does: Creates a new image artifact from the current subject and project context.
symbols.description: generate builds a context (using MS_VI_BRAIN if available), creates an image artifact through MS_PRODUCE or MS_ARTIFACTS with status 'candidate', shows a toast, clears the input, and bumps the parent to refresh the grid.

symbols.does: Increments a dummy state counter to force re-render of the workspace.
symbols.description: bump is a closure around setBump in GenerateWorkspace that triggers a React re-render so downstream resolution results and artifact grids update after external registry changes.
